Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Description: When can you withdraw from SSY? Partial withdrawal at 18, maturity at 21 years, premature closure.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_LearnText: Enter SSY balance, girl's age, and account year. See max partial withdrawal for education/marriage (≈50% after age 18 and year 2), premature closure, or full maturity at 21. Educational only — confirm with Post Office.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Simplified rules. Real partial withdrawal uses balance at end of preceding FY and purpose proofs.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_YearHint: Year 1 = first year of the account.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Note_TooEarlyYear: Account must complete year 1 before partial or premature withdrawal (simplified).
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Note_TooYoung: Partial withdrawal for education/marriage typically needs girl age ≥ 18.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Note_PartialOk: Partial withdrawal ≈ 50% of balance for education/marriage (illustrative).
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Note_PrematureOk: Premature closure may allow full balance; interest rate cut is not modelled here.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Note_Mature: At age 21, the account can be closed for the full balance (maturity).
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_ScenarioSubtitle: Education vs premature vs later age
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_ExampleStep_1: Balance = ₹5,00,000, age = 18, account year = 10, purpose = education.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_ExampleStep_4: Premature mode would show full balance (rate cut not modelled).
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_ExampleStep_5: At age 21, full maturity withdrawal applies.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Step_1: Enter balance, girl's age, and account year.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Step_4: Compare scenarios and confirm with Post Office.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much you can withdraw from an SSY account under simplified rules.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: SSY has strict age and purpose rules; knowing the cap avoids surprises.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Planning higher education or marriage expenses from an SSY balance.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Goal_1: See max partial vs premature amounts
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Ssy_When: To project maturity corpus, not withdrawals.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For bank deposit maturity, not SSY.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows SSY partial, premature, and maturity caps in one educational view.
Tool_ssy_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Know the SSY withdrawal gate before you plan.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Description: Compare two job offers CTC to in-hand. Hidden benefits valuation and negotiation leverage.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Subtitle: Current vs offer CTC → hike % and counter
Tool_salary_negotiation_LearnText: Enter current CTC, offer CTC, and your desired hike %. See the offer's implied hike, a suggested counter CTC, and whether the offer is below, meets, or above your ask.
Tool_salary_negotiation_FormulaNote: CTC comparison only — no tax, variable pay, or benefits modelling.
Tool_salary_negotiation_DesiredHint: Target increase over your current CTC for the counter suggestion.
Tool_salary_negotiation_ScenarioSubtitle: Same offer vs lower / base / higher desired hike
Tool_salary_negotiation_ExampleStep_4: Offer is below the counter → room to negotiate.
Tool_salary_negotiation_ExampleStep_5: Compare ±5% ask scenarios before you reply.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Step_3: Compute offer hike % and counter CTC.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Purpose: Compare an offer to your current CTC and a target hike.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_WhyKnow: A clear counter number helps you negotiate with confidence.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_RealLife: Responding to a job offer or internal promotion letter.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Goal_1: See implied hike on the offer
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Goal_2: Get a counter CTC from your desired %
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Alt_Hike_Wh: When you only need new CTC from a hike %.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Alt_Tax_Whe: To estimate tax on the new CTC.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Differentia: FinYatra pairs offer hike % with a desired-hike counter in one view.
Tool_salary_negotiation_Info_Motto: Know your number before you negotiate.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Description: 26 weeks maternity leave and pay. ESI vs non-ESI, adoption, and surrogacy entitlements.
Tool_maternity_benefit_LearnText: Enter average daily wage and leave days (default 182 = 26 weeks). See total maternity benefit under a simplified Maternity Benefit Act model. Confirm eligibility and ESI rules with your employer.
Tool_maternity_benefit_FormulaNote: Simplified: benefit = average daily wage × days. Does not model ESI, adoption, or surrogacy variants.
Tool_maternity_benefit_WageHint: Often average of last 3 months' wages ÷ working days.
Tool_maternity_benefit_DaysHint: Default 182 days = 26 weeks (first two children, simplified).
Tool_maternity_benefit_ScenarioSubtitle: 12 weeks vs your input vs 26 weeks
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_Purpose: Estimate maternity leave pay from average daily wage and days.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_WhyKnow: Helps plan cash flow during maternity leave.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_RealLife: Checking expected maternity pay before leave.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_Alt_Leave_Wh: For encashing unused leave, not maternity.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_Alt_Gratuity: For exit gratuity, not leave pay.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows a clear wage × days maternity estimate with 12/26 week scenarios.
Tool_maternity_benefit_Info_Motto: Plan leave pay before leave starts.
Tool_post_office_td_Description: Post Office Time Deposit maturity at 6.9-7.5%. Tax impact and comparison with bank FD and NSC.
Tool_post_office_td_LearnText: Enter deposit and choose 1, 2, 3, or 5 year tenure. Illustrative rates (6.9–7.5%) compound quarterly. Compare maturity across tenures. Confirm live rates with India Post.
Tool_post_office_td_FormulaNote: Rates are illustrative. Interest is taxable; 5-year TD may qualify under 80C separately.
Tool_post_office_td_TenureHint: Illustrative Post Office TD rate table.
Tool_post_office_td_Step_3: Apply table rate with quarterly compounding.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_Purpose: Estimate Post Office TD maturity with illustrative rates.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_WhyKnow: Tenure choice changes both rate and compounding periods.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_RealLife: Parking a lumpsum in India Post TD.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For bank FD with custom rate/frequency.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_Alt_Mis_When: For monthly income, not lumpsum maturity.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra uses an illustrative PO TD rate table with quarterly compounding and tenure compare.
Tool_post_office_td_Info_Motto: Pick the tenure — then check the live rate.
Tool_home_renovation_Description: Renovation cost estimator by sq ft. Finance options and Section 24(b) tax benefit.
Tool_home_renovation_Subtitle: Cost categories sum or renovation loan EMI
Tool_home_renovation_LearnText: Choose cost mode to sum civil, electrical, plumbing, painting, furniture, and misc with a contingency %. Or switch to loan mode for EMI on amount, rate, and tenure.
Tool_home_renovation_FormulaNote: Educational estimate only — no Section 24(b) modelling in this version.
Tool_home_renovation_ExampleStep_4: Loan mode: EMI on chosen amount, rate, tenure.
Tool_home_renovation_Step_2: Enter category amounts or loan inputs.
Tool_home_renovation_Info_Purpose: Estimate renovation spend by category or loan EMI.
Tool_home_renovation_Info_WhyKnow: Budgets overrun without a contingency buffer.
Tool_home_renovation_Info_RealLife: Planning a flat renovation or top-up loan.
Tool_home_renovation_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra combines renovation category totals and loan EMI in one tool.
Tool_home_renovation_Info_Motto: Budget the work — then price the loan.
Tool_section_80tta_Description: Tax saving on savings account interest. ₹10K deduction (80TTA) and ₹50K for seniors (80TTB).
Tool_section_80tta_LearnText: Enter savings (or deposit) interest and your tax slab. Toggle senior citizen for 80TTB (₹50,000) vs 80TTA (₹10,000). See eligible deduction, taxable interest above the limit, and estimated tax saving.
Tool_section_80tta_FormulaNote: Old-regime style illustration. 80TTA is for non-seniors on savings interest; 80TTB for seniors covers savings and deposit interest (simplified here).
Tool_section_80tta_ExampleStep_5: As senior (80TTB), full ₹15,000 would be eligible.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_Purpose: Estimate deduction and tax saving on savings interest.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_WhyKnow: Interest above the limit is still taxable.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_RealLife: Filing ITR with bank savings interest.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_Goal_2: Estimate tax saving at your slab
Tool_section_80tta_Info_Alt_80c_When: For ELSS, PPF, life insurance, etc.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra toggles 80TTA vs 80TTB limits and shows tax saving at your slab.
Tool_section_80tta_Info_Motto: Cap the interest deduction — then see the saving.
